title: The security standard that could prevent a costly mistake with AI in hospitality
excerpt: There's a global standard setting rules for organizational AI
content_html:
Most business leaders are aware that AI adoption in their organizations has moved faster than the governance around it. When we surveyed 250 finance decision makers in mid-market organizations, we found that 83% of teams were already using AI, yet only 53% had a formal framework for its safe use.

If finance - a function with some of the highest standards for data accuracy and compliance - is operating with that kind of governance gap, it is reasonable to ask whether other departments across the business look any different.

Chief of Staff at iplicit.

That gap represents a risk - and it's a risk that sits squarely with the business and its leadership. So how do you encourage innovation when it comes to AI without losing control of the risks it brings?

There is an international standard designed specifically to address this: ISO/IEC 42001. It is not a compliance exercise to tick a box; it is a practical framework for governing AI responsibly, with direct implications for how business leaders evaluate the software they rely on.

For SaaS businesses, ISO 27001, the standard for information security management systems, has become the norm. It's a key requirement, providing assurance to customers about how the business ensures Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability for the data it hosts and processes on their behalf.

ISO/IEC 42001, published in 2023, is its counterpart for AI: the first international standard governing how organizations develop, deploy and oversee AI systems. Whether or not you pursue certification yourself, it should be a key reference point when evaluating any AI-powered software you use.

Why we need a standard for AI security

It's not even four years since the public launch of ChatGPT heralded the boom in use of generative AI. Not only has the technology moved at an incredible pace since then but so has its adoption in business.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

We've all been told that AI will transform productivity and change the world of work forever. So it's not surprising that many organizations have been racing to buy licenses and get people using it. In organizations that haven't done so, it's likely that staff are using it on the side anyway - which creates another problem: "Shadow IT".

"Shadow" AI use seems to be rife. Among the finance decision makers we surveyed, in almost half (46%) of organizations where AI hadn't been officially adopted, people were using AI assistants anyway and 30% were using AI-powered forecasting and analysis. It would be surprising if the picture looked much better elsewhere in the business.

So it's worth asking: where is the data we have spent so much effort protecting, through ISO standards such as ISO 27001, now going - and do I have visibility and control over it?

The scale of ungoverned AI use matters in any organization. ISO 42001 provides a structured way to ask the right questions, whether you are reviewing your own internal AI use or evaluating a software vendor.

How ISO 42001 helps business leaders

Certain parts of an organization have particularly high standards for data accuracy and integrity - finance teams producing regulatory reporting, legal teams managing case records, HR functions handling sensitive employee data. ISO 42001 helps ensure those standards are reflected in the AI tools and processes you use, whatever your context.

Transparency: ISO 42001 requires that AI outputs can be explained and traced. Whether AI is producing a report, performing an automated workflow or flagging an anomaly, you as the human in the loop should be able to explain what the system did and why. That human in the loop is a key component of the standard.

Accountability: ISO 42001 stresses clear ownership of AI systems and their outputs. That means the use of any AI in business-critical workflows should have a defined owner who is responsible for its performance and governance.

Risk management: ISO 42001 requires ongoing risk assessment for AI systems over and above those in place for ISO 27001. This doesn't usurp what you have currently in place. It complements current risk evaluation through a focus on AI and the implementation of controls to help manage that identified risk.

That means identifying what could go wrong, how likely that event is and ensuring the right controls are in place to mitigate it. Those risks might include data accuracy, model performance degrading, security of sensitive business data and the risk of AI acting on outputs that have not been adequately verified.

The questions you should ask

ISO 42001 offers you a useful way into important conversations with software vendors, whether or not they've achieved formal certification. It prompts some important questions.

- How are the vendor's AI systems developed, tested and monitored? The ideal response would show documented processes for keeping things accurate and trustworthy, with the human in the loop clearly built into the processes.

- How does the AI product produce its output? What happens when an output is incorrect or unexpected? It's worth understanding whether the AI outputs come from a layer bolted onto a core system and drawing on verified data from that system - or whether they are generated predictively, the way a large language model would work.

- How does the vendor manage the risk of AI model performance changing over time? One of the frustrations of using AI is that LLMs can become less good at a task they did well before. You need to know your vendor is on top of this issue.

- What accountability exists within the vendor's organization for these AI capabilities? You need a relationship with a vendor that's prepared to take responsibility for its product and the data that flows from it.

- Does the vendor use your data to train or improve its AI models? This is a question that more business leaders are asking, and rightly so. Your data should never be used to improve a third-party model. Look for vendors who operate a zero-retention policy, meaning your data is used only in a live, read-only state and is never fed back into AI training processes.

However capable AI gets, it will not be replacing human decision-making and accountability at the top of organizations in the foreseeable future. That remains the job of leaders who need to know they are putting their names to decisions grounded in complete, accurate and trustworthy data from their own systems.

ISO 42001 is the mechanism by which you can hold AI to the same standards of accuracy, transparency and accountability that rigorous organizations have always required. It will not slow down AI adoption. But it will ensure that adoption does not come at the expense of the controls that protect your organization and your reputation.

We've featured the best AI chatbot for business.

This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.

The views expressed here are those of the author and are not necessarily those of TechRadarPro or Future plc. If you are interested in contributing find out more here: https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

blocks.html_en: - How are the vendor's AI systems developed, tested and monitored? The ideal response would show documented processes for keeping things accurate and trustworthy, with the human in the loop clearly built into the processes.

sentences.zh: 当输出不正确或意外时会发生什么？

sentences.en: It's worth understanding whether the AI outputs come from a layer bolted onto a core system and drawing on verified data from that system - or whether they are generated predictively, the way a large language model would work.
sentences.zh: 值得了解的是，人工智能输出是否来自固定在核心系统上并利用该系统经过验证的数据的层，或者它们是否是预测性生成的，就像大型语言模型的工作方式一样。

blocks.html_en: - How does the vendor manage the risk of AI model performance changing over time? One of the frustrations of using AI is that LLMs can become less good at a task they did well before. You need to know your vendor is on top of this issue.

sentences.en: - How does the vendor manage the risk of AI model performance changing over time?
sentences.zh: - 供应商如何管理人工智能模型性能随时间变化的风险？

sentences.en: One of the frustrations of using AI is that LLMs can become less good at a task they did well before.
sentences.zh: 使用人工智能的一个挫折是法学硕士可能会变得不太擅长他们以前擅长的任务。

sentences.en: You need to know your vendor is on top of this issue.
sentences.zh: 您需要知道您的供应商正在解决这个问题。

blocks.html_en: - What accountability exists within the vendor's organization for these AI capabilities? You need a relationship with a vendor that's prepared to take responsibility for its product and the data that flows from it.

sentences.en: - What accountability exists within the vendor's organization for these AI capabilities?
sentences.zh: - 供应商组织内对这些人工智能功能有哪些责任？

sentences.en: You need a relationship with a vendor that's prepared to take responsibility for its product and the data that flows from it.
sentences.zh: 您需要与愿意对其产品及其产生的数据负责的供应商建立关系。

blocks.html_en: - Does the vendor use your data to train or improve its AI models? This is a question that more business leaders are asking, and rightly so. Your data should never be used to improve a third-party model. Look for vendors who operate a zero-retention policy, meaning your data is used only in a live, read-only state and is never fed back into AI training processes.

sentences.en: - Does the vendor use your data to train or improve its AI models?
sentences.zh: - 供应商是否使用您的数据来训练或改进其人工智能模型？

sentences.en: This is a question that more business leaders are asking, and rightly so.
sentences.zh: 这是更多商界领袖提出的问题，也是理所应当的。

sentences.en: Your data should never be used to improve a third-party model.
sentences.zh: 您的数据永远不应该用于改进第三方模型。

sentences.en: Look for vendors who operate a zero-retention policy, meaning your data is used only in a live, read-only state and is never fed back into AI training processes.
sentences.zh: 寻找实行零保留政策的供应商，这意味着您的数据仅在实时、只读状态下使用，并且永远不会反馈到人工智能培训过程中。

blocks.html_en: However capable AI gets, it will not be replacing human decision-making and accountability at the top of organizations in the foreseeable future. That remains the job of leaders who need to know they are putting their names to decisions grounded in complete, accurate and trustworthy data from their own systems.

sentences.en: However capable AI gets, it will not be replacing human decision-making and accountability at the top of organizations in the foreseeable future.
sentences.zh: 无论人工智能多么强大，在可预见的未来，它都不会取代组织高层的人类决策和问责制。

sentences.en: That remains the job of leaders who need to know they are putting their names to decisions grounded in complete, accurate and trustworthy data from their own systems.
sentences.zh: 这仍然是领导者的工作，他们需要知道自己的决策是基于来自自己系统的完整、准确和值得信赖的数据。

blocks.html_en: ISO 42001 is the mechanism by which you can hold AI to the same standards of accuracy, transparency and accountability that rigorous organizations have always required. It will not slow down AI adoption. But it will ensure that adoption does not come at the expense of the controls that protect your organization and your reputation.

sentences.en: ISO 42001 is the mechanism by which you can hold AI to the same standards of accuracy, transparency and accountability that rigorous organizations have always required.
sentences.zh: ISO 42001 是一种机制，您可以通过该机制让 AI 达到严格的组织始终要求的相同的准确性、透明度和问责制标准。

sentences.en: It will not slow down AI adoption.
sentences.zh: 它不会减慢人工智能的采用。

sentences.en: But it will ensure that adoption does not come at the expense of the controls that protect your organization and your reputation.
sentences.zh: 但它将确保采用不会以牺牲保护您的组织和声誉的控制为代价。

blocks.html_en: We've featured the best AI chatbot for business.

sentences.en: We've featured the best AI chatbot for business.
sentences.zh: 我们推出了最适合商业的人工智能聊天机器人。

sentences.en: This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.
sentences.zh: 本文是 TechRadar Pro Perspectives 的一部分，这是我们的频道，旨在介绍当今技术行业中最优秀、最聪明的人才。

sentences.en: The views expressed here are those of the author and are not necessarily those of TechRadarPro or Future plc.
sentences.zh: 本文表达的观点仅代表作者的观点，并不一定代表 TechRadarPro 或 Future plc 的观点。

sentences.en: If you are interested in contributing find out more here: https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit
sentences.zh: 如果您有兴趣贡献，请在此处了解更多信息：https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

content_html_zh:
大多数企业领导者都意识到，其组织中人工智能的采用速度快于围绕其的治理速度。 当我们调查中端市场组织的 250 名财务决策者时，我们发现 83% 的团队已经在使用人工智能，但只有 53% 的团队拥有安全使用人工智能的正式框架。

如果财务部门（在数据准确性和合规性方面具有最高标准的职能部门）在这种治理差距的情况下运作，那么有理由询问整个企业的其他部门是否看起来有什么不同。

内隐部参谋长。

这种差距代表着一种风险，而这种风险与企业及其领导层息息相关。 那么，如何在人工智能方面鼓励创新，同时又不失去对其带来的风险的控制呢？

有一个专门为解决这个问题而设计的国际标准：ISO/IEC 42001。 勾选方框并不是一项合规活动；而是一项合规活动。它是一个负责任地管理人工智能的实用框架，对企业领导者如何评估他们所依赖的软件有直接影响。

对于 SaaS 企业来说，信息安全管理系统标准 ISO 27001 已成为常态。 这是一项关键要求，可以向客户保证企业如何确保其代表客户托管和处理的数据的机密性、完整性和可用性。

ISO/IEC 42001 于 2023 年发布，是人工智能的对应标准：第一个管理组织如何开发、部署和监督人工智能系统的国际标准。 无论您是否自己寻求认证，它都应该成为评估您使用的任何人工智能软件时的关键参考点。

为什么我们需要人工智能安全标准

ChatGPT 的公开发布预示着生成式人工智能的使用热潮，这还不到四年。 从那时起，这项技术不仅以令人难以置信的速度发展，而且在商业中的采用也是如此。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

我们都被告知，人工智能将永远改变生产力并改变工作世界。 因此，许多组织竞相购买许可证并让人们使用它也就不足为奇了。 在尚未这样做的组织中，员工很可能会在业余时间使用它，这会产生另一个问题：“影子 IT”。

“影子”人工智能的使用似乎很普遍。 在我们调查的财务决策者中，近一半（46%）尚未正式采用人工智能的组织中，人们无论如何都在使用人工智能助手，而 30% 的组织正在使用人工智能驱动的预测和分析。 如果行业其他地方的情况看起来好得多，那将是令人惊讶的。

因此，值得一问的是：我们花费大量精力通过 ISO 27001 等 ISO 标准保护的数据现在去哪儿了？我是否可以对其进行可见性和控制？

不受监管的人工智能使用规模对任何组织都很重要。 无论您是在审查自己的内部人工智能使用情况还是评估软件供应商，ISO 42001 都提供了一种结构化的方法来提出正确的问题。

ISO 42001 如何帮助企业领导者

组织的某些部分对数据准确性和完整性有特别高的标准——财务团队生成监管报告、法律团队管理案件记录、人力资源职能部门处理敏感的员工数据。 ISO 42001 有助于确保这些标准反映在您使用的人工智能工具和流程中，无论您的环境如何。

透明度：ISO 42001 要求 AI 输出可以解释和追踪。 无论人工智能是生成报告、执行自动化工作流程还是标记异常，作为循环中的人，您都应该能够解释系统做了什么以及为什么这样做。 循环中的人员是该标准的关键组成部分。

问责制：ISO 42001 强调人工智能系统及其输出的明确所有权。 这意味着在关键业务工作流程中使用任何人工智能都应该有一个明确的所有者，负责其性能和治理。

风险管理：除了 ISO 27001 的风险管理之外，ISO 42001 还要求对人工智能系统进行持续的风险评估。 这不会篡夺您当前所拥有的。 它通过关注人工智能和实施控制来补充当前的风险评估，以帮助管理已识别的风险。

这意味着要确定可能出现问题的地方、该事件发生的可能性有多大，并确保采取正确的控制措施来缓解问题。 这些风险可能包括数据准确性、模型性能下降、敏感业务数据的安全性以及人工智能作用于未经充分验证的输出的风险。

你应该问的问题

ISO 42001 为您提供了一种与软件供应商进行重要对话的有用方法，无论他们是否已获得正式认证。 它提出了一些重要的问题。

- 供应商的人工智能系统是如何开发、测试和监控的？ 理想的响应将显示记录的流程，以确保事情准确且值得信赖，并且将人员明确纳入流程中。

- AI产品如何产生输出？ 当输出不正确或意外时会发生什么？ 值得了解的是，人工智能输出是否来自固定在核心系统上并利用该系统经过验证的数据的层，或者它们是否是预测性生成的，就像大型语言模型的工作方式一样。

- 供应商如何管理人工智能模型性能随时间变化的风险？ 使用人工智能的一个挫折是法学硕士可能会变得不太擅长他们以前擅长的任务。 您需要知道您的供应商正在解决这个问题。

- 供应商组织内对这些人工智能功能有哪些责任？ 您需要与愿意对其产品及其产生的数据负责的供应商建立关系。

- 供应商是否使用您的数据来训练或改进其人工智能模型？ 这是更多商界领袖提出的问题，也是理所应当的。 您的数据永远不应该用于改进第三方模型。 寻找实行零保留政策的供应商，这意味着您的数据仅在实时、只读状态下使用，并且永远不会反馈到人工智能培训过程中。

无论人工智能多么强大，在可预见的未来，它都不会取代组织高层的人类决策和问责制。 这仍然是领导者的工作，他们需要知道自己的决策是基于来自自己系统的完整、准确和值得信赖的数据。

ISO 42001 是一种机制，您可以通过该机制让 AI 达到严格的组织始终要求的相同的准确性、透明度和问责制标准。 它不会减慢人工智能的采用。 但它将确保采用不会以牺牲保护您的组织和声誉的控制为代价。

我们推出了最适合商业的人工智能聊天机器人。

本文是 TechRadar Pro Perspectives 的一部分，这是我们的频道，旨在介绍当今技术行业中最优秀、最聪明的人才。

本文表达的观点仅代表作者的观点，并不一定代表 TechRadarPro 或 Future plc 的观点。 如果您有兴趣贡献，请在此处了解更多信息：https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit
